prevalence.trend: stable; improved diagnosis and specialist follow-up in tertiary centers

burden.dalyPerCapita: very low (rare) but significant lifelong orthopedic/ENT/neurologic morbidity
burden.mortalityRate: Low; higher in infancy with severe airway or foramen magnum stenosis

vulnerablePopulations: children with sleep apnea/recurrent ear disease
patients lacking access to orthopedics and neurosurgery
epidemiology.diagnosticCriteria: Clinical-radiographic diagnosis with confirmatory FGFR3 testing; surveillance for foramen magnum stenosis, hydrocephalus, sleep apnea, spinal stenosis and hearing issues

publicHealthSystem.screeningCoverage: no screening; diagnosis usually at birth/early childhood
publicHealthSystem.controlRate: Variable; best with multidisciplinary care pathways

regionalSpecificities.diagnosisCriteria: Genetics/orthopedics confirmation concentrated in tertiary hospitals
regionalSpecificities.treatmentProtocol: Multidisciplinary monitoring (orthopedics, ENT, sleep medicine, neurosurgery), physiotherapy and mobility support, genetic counseling
regionalSpecificities.regionalChallenges: Limited specialist availability outside major cities and delays for neurosurgical assessment in some regions
regionalSpecificities.strongPoints: Strong tertiary pediatric centers can coordinate multidisciplinary care when accessed
